# auto-skeleton-vue

> Auto-generated skeleton loaders for Vue 3 that mirror a component's real rendered layout. Wrap any component in `<AutoSkeleton :loading>` and it produces a skeleton derived from the component's actual DOM — no separate skeleton component to author, and it stays in sync automatically because it is captured from real renders.

## How it works

The skeleton is always "one render behind":

1. When `loading` is `false`, the real component renders. After paint, AutoSkeleton walks its DOM and captures the layout into a compact, serializable descriptor (text becomes per-line bars, images become blocks), keyed by component identity plus viewport-width bucket.
2. When `loading` is `true`, it replays the cached descriptor — a pixel-accurate skeleton of the last real render.
3. On the very first load (no capture yet) it shows a small generic fallback. Enabling `persist` caches geometry in localStorage across reloads and skips that flash.

Because every successful render overwrites the cache, changing a component automatically updates its skeleton. There is no sync step, and the child is never force-rendered without data.

## Usage

```vue
<AutoSkeleton :loading="isLoading">
  <UserCard :user="user" />
</AutoSkeleton>
```

```ts
import { createApp } from 'vue'
import { createAutoSkeleton } from 'auto-skeleton-vue'
import 'auto-skeleton-vue/style.css'

createApp(App)
  .use(createAutoSkeleton({ persist: true, animation: true }))
  .mount('#app')
```

## Docs

- Getting started: https://oleksiizlotnik.github.io/AutoSkeleton/guide/getting-started
- How it works: https://oleksiizlotnik.github.io/AutoSkeleton/guide/how-it-works
- Configuration & theming: https://oleksiizlotnik.github.io/AutoSkeleton/guide/configuration
- Caching & persistence: https://oleksiizlotnik.github.io/AutoSkeleton/guide/caching
- Live demo: https://oleksiizlotnik.github.io/AutoSkeleton/guide/demo
